political.s: Hormel Foods does not use corporate funds for direct contributions to political candidates or parties. The company engages in lobbying through trade associations on food industry issues including FDA regulations, USDA rules, and trade policy. Lobbying spending was approximately $490,000 in 2024.

charity.s: Hormel Foods donated approximately $22.6 million in charitable and in-kind donations in 2024, focusing on food security, education, and community support. The company also distributes product donations to hunger relief organizations globally and contributed $340,000 through plant donation programs to local charities.
charity.focus: Food security, education, community development, hunger relief

environment.s: Hormel Foods committed to reducing operational greenhouse gas emissions 50% by 2030 and supply chain emissions 27.5% by 2030 from 2019 baseline. The company achieved 92% renewable energy sourcing in 2024, reduced packaging by 1 million+ pounds, and saved 73 million gallons of water. The company has received EPA and sustainability awards for its environmental efforts.

labor.s: Hormel Foods emphasizes workplace safety with award-winning programs and conducts approximately 1,000 monthly safety assessments. However, the company has a history of labor disputes and mixed workplace culture reviews. The company states zero human rights violations in 2022 and provides comprehensive benefits including health centers and tuition-free education programs.

dei.s: Hormel Foods states commitment to equal employment opportunity and non-discrimination across protected classes. The company includes DEI metrics in executive compensation and provides diversity and inclusion training. However, the company disclosed underperformance on environmental, social and governance goals.

animals.s: Hormel Foods has developed industry-leading animal welfare standards in conjunction with livestock care experts and veterinarians focused on humane treatment. The company states it works with animal behavior experts to ensure animals are raised in healthy environments with care and respect throughout their lives.

guns.s: As a food company, Hormel Foods does not engage in firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: Limited publicly available information on Hormel Foods' comprehensive data privacy practices and consumer data protection policies.

execPay.s: CEO James Snee earned approximately $7.1 million in total compensation in 2024. The CEO pay ratio was approximately 188:1 based on 2021 data, with median employee pay around $50,369. Executive compensation emphasizes performance-based incentives tied to earnings per share and financial metrics.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
